mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives short-term S&P 500 returns positive over the next week during economic recovery periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a walk-forward validation over 18 months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of contracting real GDP and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The co-occurrence of falling GDPC1 and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with the interaction term showing no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 0.5% quarterly, corporate earnings momentum stalls and risk premiums widen, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 0.5%, or the predictive signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward testing.
